what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 stroke-only SVG lock icon using currentColor, composed of a rounded rectangular body and an arched shackle path.
observations: Uses viewBox "0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke-width "1.5" with round line caps and joins.
Draws the lock body as a rounded rect at x=5, y=11, width=14, height=10, rx=2.
Draws the shackle as a path from y=11 up to y=7 with a 4-unit-radius arch.
standouts: Color is inherited from surrounding text or CSS through currentColor.
Geometry is centered within a standard 24px icon canvas.
conventions: Follows common outline icon SVG conventions: 24x24 viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Uses inline SVG elements without IDs, classes, metadata, or embedded styling.
concerns: No title, role, or aria attributes are embedded, so accessibility semantics depend on the consuming context.
notes: A compact outline lock icon asset intended to inherit size context and color from its usage environment.
questions: Should this SVG be treated as decorative by default or labeled by the consuming UI?
Should width and height remain fixed at 24, or should sizing be fully consumer-controlled?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and content align: this file defines a lock icon SVG.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a simple, theme-compatible lock/security glyph for interface use.
contribution: Contributes a lightweight lock/security icon with inherited coloring, standard 24px geometry, and simple stroke-based construction that a fused system could keep as a reusable visual asset.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor outline SVG lock icon made from one rounded rectangle and one shackle path.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing lock, privacy, security, or restricted access states in a design system icon set.
